meta.trust_note: CAID carries no trust semantics. A registered type defines WHAT must be inside the digested content, never whether the action was authorized, executed, safe, or wise. See GOVERNANCE.md.
meta.entry_schema_note: Each entry in 'types' conforms to the normative type entry schema in DESIGN.md section 3. Local (private) definition files MUST use the same entry schema; there is no reserved private-use syntax. Field types are the closed v1 set: string, amount-string, digest, enum, timestamp, integer, boolean, object, array. 'values_ref' on an enum either names an external code set or is an inline pipe-separated list prefixed 'inline:'. Date-only values use type string with an ISO 8601 date note; type timestamp is always full RFC 3339 UTC with Z.

risk_classes.irreversible-financial: Money or financial obligation moves or is created and cannot be unilaterally recalled.
risk_classes.irreversible-data: Data or state is destroyed, or leaves a boundary it cannot be pulled back across.
risk_classes.irreversible-access: Access control, credentials, or key material change in a way that is hard to fully claw back.
risk_classes.external-communication: Content is transmitted or published to parties outside the operator's control.
risk_classes.safety-critical: Direct impact on human health, safety, or live critical service delivery.
risk_classes.legal-effect: Creates, releases, or submits a legally binding obligation or record.

types.summary: Release of a payment instruction to settlement.

required_fields.name: amount
required_fields.notes: decimal string, no exponent, no leading '+', no thousands separators

required_fields.name: beneficiary_account
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized account identifier; normalization stated by the issuing system of record

types.digest_notes: amounts never renormalized after signing; the system of record's form is canonical

types.summary: Return of funds against a previously settled payment.

required_fields.name: original_payment_id
required_fields.notes: identifier of the settled payment in the issuing system of record

required_fields.name: amount
required_fields.notes: refunded amount; may be less than or equal to the original

required_fields.name: destination_account
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized destination account identifier

types.digest_notes: amount string taken verbatim from the refund instruction; partial refunds against the same original produce distinct CAIDs

types.summary: Execution of a batch of payout instructions as a single unit.

required_fields.name: total_amount
required_fields.notes: sum of all items in the batch, in batch currency

required_fields.name: item_count
required_fields.notes: number of individual payout items in the batch

required_fields.name: manifest_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the canonical batch manifest listing every item; binds the batch content without inlining beneficiary data

optional_fields.name: value_date
optional_fields.notes: ISO 8601 date, YYYY-MM-DD

types.digest_notes: the manifest, not this object, carries per-item detail; changing any item changes manifest_digest and therefore the CAID

types.summary: Origination of a wire transfer (e.g. Fedwire, CHIPS, SWIFT).

required_fields.name: debtor_account
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized debtor account identifier (e.g. IBAN uppercased, no spaces)

required_fields.name: creditor_account
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized creditor account identifier

required_fields.name: creditor_agent_bic
required_fields.notes: ISO 9362 BIC, 8 or 11 characters, uppercase; BICs are public routing identifiers

required_fields.name: end_to_end_id
required_fields.notes: ISO 20022 EndToEndIdentification supplied by the originator

optional_fields.name: requested_execution_date
optional_fields.notes: ISO 8601 date, YYYY-MM-DD

types.digest_notes: account normalization is the originating institution's stated rule; the digest binds the normalized form

types.summary: Origination of an ACH debit entry against a receiver's account.

required_fields.name: currency
required_fields.notes: USD for domestic ACH

required_fields.name: sec_code
required_fields.values_ref: Nacha Operating Rules, Standard Entry Class codes (PPD, CCD, WEB, TEL, ...)

required_fields.name: receiver_account
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized receiver DFI account number

required_fields.name: receiver_routing
required_fields.notes: 9-digit ABA routing number of the receiving DFI; routing numbers are public

required_fields.name: company_entry_description
required_fields.notes: 10-character Nacha company entry description as it will appear to the receiver

optional_fields.name: effective_entry_date
optional_fields.notes: ISO 8601 date, YYYY-MM-DD

types.digest_notes: account number normalized per the ODFI's stated rule before hashing; amount verbatim from the entry detail record

types.summary: Grant of a role or permission set to a principal within a scope.

required_fields.name: principal
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized principal identifier (user id, service account id); avoids raw identity in the object

required_fields.name: role
required_fields.notes: role identifier as defined by the target system, e.g. roles/owner or arn:aws:iam::...:role/name

required_fields.name: scope
required_fields.notes: resource, project, folder, or organization the grant applies to

optional_fields.name: condition
optional_fields.notes: condition expression if the target system supports conditional grants

types.digest_notes: principal normalization (case, provider prefix) is stated by the identity system of record

types.summary: Revocation of a role or permission set from a principal within a scope.

required_fields.name: principal
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized principal identifier

types.digest_notes: same principal normalization rule as iam.role.grant.1 so grant and revoke over the same principal hash identically

types.summary: Deletion of a user account from a directory or tenant.

required_fields.name: user_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized user identifier

required_fields.name: directory
required_fields.notes: tenant, directory, or realm identifier

required_fields.name: cascade_owned_resources
required_fields.notes: true if resources owned by the user are deleted with the account

optional_fields.name: retention_until
optional_fields.notes: soft-delete retention deadline if the directory supports one

types.digest_notes: user identifier normalized per the directory's stated rule (typically lowercase UPN or immutable object id)

types.summary: Creation of a cryptographic key in a key store or HSM.

required_fields.name: key_id
required_fields.notes: key identifier or full key resource path in the target store

required_fields.name: algorithm
required_fields.values_ref: IANA JOSE algorithms registry (RFC 7518) or the store's documented algorithm list

required_fields.name: key_ops
required_fields.notes: permitted operations; values from RFC 7517 section 4.3 key_ops (sign, verify, encrypt, decrypt, wrapKey, unwrapKey, deriveKey, deriveBits)

required_fields.name: exportable
required_fields.notes: true if key material may leave the store

types.digest_notes: no key material or public key bytes in this object; the object identifies the creation request, not the resulting key

types.summary: Deletion or scheduled destruction of a cryptographic key. Destroying key material can permanently orphan ciphertext.

required_fields.name: key_store
required_fields.notes: identifier of the KMS, HSM partition, or vault holding the key

required_fields.name: destroy_material
required_fields.notes: true for irreversible destruction of key material, false for disable-only

optional_fields.name: scheduled_destruction_at
optional_fields.notes: end of the pending-deletion window if the store supports one

types.digest_notes: key_id verbatim as the store names it; no material fields carry key bytes

types.summary: Rotation of a cryptographic key to a new version.

required_fields.name: new_algorithm
required_fields.values_ref: IANA JOSE algorithms registry (RFC 7518) or the store's documented algorithm list
required_fields.notes: algorithm of the new version; equal to the old algorithm for a like-for-like rotation

optional_fields.name: retire_old_at
optional_fields.notes: when the prior version stops being usable for its primary operation

types.digest_notes: identifies the rotation request; new version identifiers are assigned by the store after execution and are not in this object

types.summary: Disabling a multi-factor authentication factor on a user account.

required_fields.name: user_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized user identifier

required_fields.name: factor_type
required_fields.values_ref: inline: totp | webauthn | sms | voice | push | recovery-codes | all

optional_fields.name: reenroll_required
optional_fields.notes: true if the user must re-enroll a factor at next sign-in

types.digest_notes: factor_type 'all' means every enrolled factor; a per-factor disable names the single factor type

types.summary: Deletion of a DNS resource record set from a zone.

required_fields.name: zone
required_fields.notes: zone apex as a fully qualified lowercase domain name without trailing dot

required_fields.name: record_name
required_fields.notes: owner name, fully qualified, lowercase

required_fields.name: record_type
required_fields.values_ref: IANA DNS Resource Record TYPEs (RFC 1035 and successors)

required_fields.name: rdata
required_fields.notes: presentation-format RDATA of the record set being deleted; DNS data is public

optional_fields.name: ttl
optional_fields.notes: TTL in seconds of the record set at deletion time

types.digest_notes: names lowercased and fully qualified before digesting; rdata in the zone's presentation format verbatim

types.summary: Registrar transfer of a domain to a gaining registrar.

required_fields.name: domain
required_fields.notes: lowercase fully qualified domain name

required_fields.name: gaining_registrar
required_fields.notes: IANA registrar name or ID of the gaining registrar

required_fields.name: auth_code_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the EPP authorization code; the code itself is secret and never appears in the object

types.digest_notes: binds the transfer to the specific auth code issued for it without disclosing the code

types.summary: Termination of a compute instance or virtual machine.

required_fields.name: instance_id
required_fields.notes: provider-assigned instance identifier

required_fields.name: provider
required_fields.notes: cloud or virtualization provider, e.g. aws, gcp, azure, vsphere

required_fields.name: delete_attached_storage
required_fields.notes: true if non-root volumes are deleted with the instance

types.digest_notes: instance_id verbatim as the provider assigns it

types.summary: Deletion of a compute, database, or orchestration cluster.

required_fields.name: delete_persistent_volumes
required_fields.notes: true if persistent volumes and their data are destroyed with the cluster

optional_fields.name: node_count
optional_fields.notes: node count at deletion time, as a blast-radius signal

types.digest_notes: cluster_id verbatim as the control plane names it

types.summary: Creation of a firewall or security-group rule that permits traffic.

required_fields.name: target
required_fields.notes: network, security group, or host the rule attaches to

required_fields.name: protocol
required_fields.values_ref: IANA Assigned Internet Protocol Numbers (tcp, udp, icmp, ...) or 'any'

required_fields.name: port_range
required_fields.notes: single port '443', range '1024-65535', or 'any'

required_fields.name: remote_cidr
required_fields.notes: CIDR the rule permits, e.g. 0.0.0.0/0; the source for ingress, the destination for egress

optional_fields.name: expires_at
optional_fields.notes: for time-boxed exceptions

types.digest_notes: CIDRs in canonical form (no host bits set); port_range verbatim from the rule request

types.summary: Making an object storage bucket publicly accessible. Once public, contents may be copied and cannot be recalled.

required_fields.name: bucket
required_fields.notes: bucket name as the provider addresses it

optional_fields.name: object_count
optional_fields.notes: object count at change time, as a blast-radius signal

optional_fields.name: prefix
optional_fields.notes: restrict the public grant to a key prefix if the provider supports it

types.digest_notes: bucket name verbatim; absence of prefix means the whole bucket

types.summary: Deletion of a dataset, table, or collection from a data store.

required_fields.name: dataset_id
required_fields.notes: fully qualified dataset, table, or collection identifier in the store's addressing scheme

required_fields.name: store
required_fields.notes: data store or warehouse identifier

required_fields.name: snapshot_before_delete
required_fields.notes: true if a recoverable snapshot is taken immediately before deletion

optional_fields.name: record_count
optional_fields.notes: approximate record count at deletion time, as a blast-radius signal

types.digest_notes: dataset_id verbatim in the store's fully qualified form

types.summary: Deletion of a backup or recovery point.

required_fields.name: backup_set
required_fields.notes: the protected resource or backup plan the recovery point belongs to

required_fields.name: backup_created_at
required_fields.notes: creation time of the recovery point being deleted

required_fields.name: is_last_recovery_point
required_fields.notes: true if no other recovery point for the protected resource will remain

types.digest_notes: is_last_recovery_point is material precisely because it distinguishes pruning from total loss of recoverability

types.summary: Override of a data retention policy, enabling earlier destruction or longer holding than the policy prescribes.

required_fields.name: scope
required_fields.notes: data class, dataset, or organizational scope the override applies to

types.digest_notes: both retention values are material so shortening and lengthening are distinguishable in the digested content

types.summary: Export of personal data outside its governed store.

required_fields.name: subject_count
required_fields.notes: number of data subjects represented in the export

required_fields.name: field_classes
required_fields.notes: PII field classes included, e.g. name, email, government-id, financial, health; the operator's classification taxonomy governs

required_fields.name: destination_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized destination identifier (URL, account, or system id)

required_fields.name: legal_basis
required_fields.values_ref: GDPR Article 6(1) lawful bases (consent, contract, legal-obligation, vital-interests, public-task, legitimate-interests) or the applicable regime's equivalent

optional_fields.name: expires_at
optional_fields.notes: agreed deletion deadline at the destination, if any

types.digest_notes: no subject-level data in the object; destination normalization stated by the exporting system

types.summary: Sending an email to recipients outside the operator's domain.

required_fields.name: recipients_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> over the newline-joined, lowercased, sorted list of recipient addresses; addresses are PII and never appear raw

required_fields.name: subject
required_fields.notes: verbatim subject line

required_fields.name: body_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the exact message body bytes as they will be sent

optional_fields.name: attachments_digest
optional_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> over the concatenated sha256 hex digests of each attachment, in attachment order

optional_fields.name: from_address
optional_fields.notes: sending address; typically a shared organizational address, not personal PII

types.digest_notes: any edit to recipients or body after CAID issuance changes the digest; that is the point

types.summary: Publishing an announcement to a channel or audience.

required_fields.name: channel
required_fields.notes: destination channel identifier, e.g. status-page, blog, changelog, in-app banner

required_fields.name: body_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the exact content bytes to be published

types.digest_notes: title is inlined for human review surfaces; the body binds by digest

types.summary: Publishing a post to a social platform account.

required_fields.name: platform
required_fields.notes: e.g. x, linkedin, mastodon, bluesky

required_fields.name: account_handle
required_fields.notes: the publishing account's public handle

required_fields.name: content_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the exact post text bytes

optional_fields.name: media_digests
optional_fields.notes: array of sha256:<lowercase hex> digests of attached media, in attachment order

optional_fields.name: in_reply_to
optional_fields.notes: platform identifier of the post being replied to

types.digest_notes: handles are public; content binds by digest so drafts and the published post are distinguishable

types.summary: Distribution of a press release through a wire or distribution service.

required_fields.name: body_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the final release body bytes

required_fields.name: distribution_service
required_fields.notes: e.g. businesswire, prnewswire, globenewswire

required_fields.name: circuits
required_fields.notes: distribution circuits or lists, e.g. national, trade-tech, emea

types.digest_notes: material-news releases may be market-moving; embargo time, when present, is material and digested

types.summary: Publishing a package version to a public or third-party registry. Most registries treat published versions as immutable.

required_fields.name: registry
required_fields.notes: e.g. npm, pypi, crates.io, maven-central, rubygems

required_fields.name: package_name
required_fields.notes: verbatim, including scope or group where applicable

required_fields.name: version
required_fields.notes: the exact version string being published, e.g. SemVer 2.0.0

required_fields.name: artifact_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the exact artifact bytes uploaded (tarball, wheel, crate)

optional_fields.name: dist_tag
optional_fields.notes: e.g. latest, beta

optional_fields.name: provenance_attached
optional_fields.notes: true if a build provenance attestation is published alongside

types.digest_notes: artifact_digest binds the exact bytes; rebuilding, even from the same source, produces a different CAID unless the build is reproducible

types.summary: Deployment of an artifact to a production environment serving live traffic.

required_fields.name: service
required_fields.notes: deployable service or application identifier

required_fields.name: environment
required_fields.notes: the production environment identifier, e.g. prod, prod-eu

required_fields.name: artifact_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the deployed artifact (image manifest, bundle, or binary)

required_fields.name: source_ref
required_fields.notes: full VCS commit hash the artifact was built from

optional_fields.name: rollback_ref
optional_fields.notes: commit hash or artifact reference the deployment would roll back to

types.digest_notes: artifact_digest, not source_ref, is the ground truth of what runs; source_ref is material for human review

types.summary: Rotation of an application secret, credential, or token to a new value.

required_fields.name: secret_id
required_fields.notes: path or identifier of the secret in the store; never the secret value

required_fields.name: consumers_notified
required_fields.notes: true if dependent systems are updated or notified as part of the rotation

optional_fields.name: invalidate_old_at
optional_fields.notes: when the prior value stops being accepted

types.digest_notes: no field carries a secret value, old or new, in any form including its hash; the object identifies the rotation event only

types.summary: Deletion of a source code repository including its history, issues, and metadata.

required_fields.name: repo
required_fields.notes: owner/name form, e.g. acme/billing-service

required_fields.name: host
required_fields.notes: e.g. github.com, gitlab.com, or a self-hosted instance hostname

optional_fields.name: archive_digest
optional_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of a final archive taken before deletion, if one was taken

optional_fields.name: fork_count
optional_fields.notes: public fork count at deletion time; forks survive deletion on most hosts

types.digest_notes: repo names lowercased per the host's rules before digesting

types.summary: Disabling branch protection rules on a repository branch.

required_fields.name: repo
required_fields.notes: owner/name form

required_fields.name: branch
required_fields.notes: branch name or protection rule pattern being disabled

optional_fields.name: reenable_at
optional_fields.notes: for time-boxed maintenance windows

optional_fields.name: rules_disabled
optional_fields.notes: the specific rules turned off, e.g. required-reviews, required-status-checks, force-push-block

types.digest_notes: absence of rules_disabled means the whole protection rule is removed

types.summary: Placement of a purchase order committing the buyer to payment.

required_fields.name: merchant_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized merchant identifier

required_fields.name: total_amount
required_fields.notes: grand total including tax and shipping, in order currency

required_fields.name: items_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the canonical line-item list (sku, quantity, unit price per line)

required_fields.name: customer_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized customer identifier

required_fields.name: fulfillment_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the canonical delivery, pickup, or digital-fulfillment target

types.digest_notes: merchant, customer, fulfillment target, and canonical line items are material; an order identifier alone is not globally sufficient

types.summary: Cancellation of a recurring subscription.

required_fields.name: customer_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized customer identifier

required_fields.name: effective_at
required_fields.notes: immediate cancellation uses the request time; end-of-period uses the period end

types.digest_notes: effective_at distinguishes cancel-now from cancel-at-period-end in the digested content

types.summary: Issuing a refund to a customer against a prior order or charge.

required_fields.name: customer_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized customer identifier

types.digest_notes: multiple partial refunds against the same order produce distinct CAIDs via distinct amounts or an issuer-added sequence field

types.summary: Removal of a listing from a public marketplace or catalog.

required_fields.name: reason_code
required_fields.values_ref: inline: policy-violation | seller-request | legal-demand | safety-recall | inventory

optional_fields.name: seller_ref
optional_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized seller identifier

types.digest_notes: reason_code is material because legal-demand and safety-recall delistings carry obligations that seller-request does not

types.summary: Execution (signing) of a contract, binding the executing party.

required_fields.name: contract_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the final contract document bytes as executed

required_fields.name: counterparty_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized counterparty legal identifier (legal name plus jurisdiction, or registry number); counterparties may be natural persons

required_fields.name: governing_law
required_fields.notes: governing jurisdiction, e.g. 'State of Delaware, USA' or 'England and Wales'

required_fields.name: effective_date
required_fields.notes: ISO 8601 date, YYYY-MM-DD

optional_fields.name: contract_value
optional_fields.notes: total contract value where a single value is stated

types.digest_notes: contract_digest is over the exact executed bytes; a redlined draft and the executed document are different CAIDs

types.summary: Submission of a filing to a regulator.

required_fields.name: regulator
required_fields.notes: e.g. SEC, FCA, BaFin, FinCEN, state insurance commissioner

required_fields.name: filing_type
required_fields.notes: form identifier in the regulator's catalog, e.g. 10-K, SAR, MDR

required_fields.name: period_end
required_fields.notes: ISO 8601 date, YYYY-MM-DD; the reporting period the filing covers

required_fields.name: document_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the exact submission package bytes

required_fields.name: filer_id
required_fields.notes: regulator-assigned filer identifier, e.g. SEC CIK; these are public identifiers

optional_fields.name: amendment_of
optional_fields.notes: accession or reference number of the filing being amended

types.digest_notes: document_digest binds the submitted bytes; an amended filing is a new action object

types.summary: Release of a legal hold, allowing normal retention and deletion to resume over the held material.

required_fields.name: matter_id
required_fields.notes: the litigation or investigation matter the hold belongs to

required_fields.name: custodian_count
required_fields.notes: number of custodians whose material is released

required_fields.name: counsel_approved
required_fields.notes: true if counsel of record approved the release

optional_fields.name: released_scope
optional_fields.notes: description of the data sources and date ranges released

types.digest_notes: custodian identities never appear in the object; scope is descriptive, custodian_count is the blast-radius signal

types.summary: Dispensing of a prescription medication to a patient.

required_fields.name: patient_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized patient identifier per the pharmacy system of record; raw patient identifiers never appear

required_fields.name: ndc_code
required_fields.notes: 11-digit NDC in 5-4-2 format identifying the exact product and package

required_fields.name: drug_name
required_fields.notes: label name for human review; ndc_code is authoritative

required_fields.name: quantity
required_fields.notes: dispensed quantity in the product's billing unit; fractional quantities occur (e.g. 7.5 mL), hence amount-string

required_fields.name: prescriber_npi
required_fields.notes: 10-digit National Provider Identifier; NPIs are public registry data

optional_fields.name: refill_number
optional_fields.notes: 0 for the original fill

optional_fields.name: rx_ref
optional_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized prescription number; prescription numbers are patient-linkable

types.digest_notes: NDC and quantity together identify what physically leaves the pharmacy; both verbatim from the fill record

types.summary: Approval of a prior authorization request for a procedure, service, or drug.

required_fields.name: patient_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized member or patient identifier

required_fields.name: service_code
required_fields.values_ref: AMA CPT or CMS HCPCS Level II code sets (or NDC for drug authorizations)

required_fields.name: authorization_number
required_fields.notes: payer-assigned authorization number

optional_fields.name: units_approved
optional_fields.notes: approved units, visits, or fills

optional_fields.name: provider_npi
optional_fields.notes: 10-digit NPI of the requesting provider; public registry data

types.digest_notes: the validity window is material; extending an authorization is a new action object

types.summary: Disclosure of protected health information to a recipient outside the disclosing covered entity.

required_fields.name: patient_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized patient identifier

required_fields.name: recipient_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized recipient identifier (organization or individual)

required_fields.name: purpose
required_fields.values_ref: 45 CFR 164.506 and 164.512 disclosure purposes (treatment, payment, health-care-operations, required-by-law, public-health, ...)

required_fields.name: record_scope
required_fields.notes: description of the records disclosed, e.g. 'encounter notes 2025-01-01 to 2025-06-30'

required_fields.name: minimum_necessary_applied
required_fields.notes: true if the minimum-necessary standard was applied; false is permitted (e.g. treatment disclosures) but must be explicit

optional_fields.name: authorization_ref
optional_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the patient authorization document where the purpose requires one

types.digest_notes: no PHI in the object itself; the object is accounting-of-disclosures material, not the disclosure

types.summary: Disbursement of a government or program benefit payment to a beneficiary.

required_fields.name: program
required_fields.notes: benefit program identifier, e.g. snap, unemployment-insurance, housing-assistance

required_fields.name: beneficiary_ref
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized beneficiary identifier; beneficiary identity is PII and never appears raw

required_fields.name: benefit_period
required_fields.notes: ISO 8601 year-month YYYY-MM, or an explicit date range, per program rules

optional_fields.name: case_id
optional_fields.notes: program case identifier where it is not itself PII-linkable outside the agency

types.digest_notes: benefit_period plus beneficiary_ref distinguishes recurring disbursements from duplicates

types.summary: Approval of a vendor invoice for payment.

required_fields.name: invoice_id
required_fields.notes: the vendor's invoice number as presented

required_fields.name: vendor_id
required_fields.notes: the payer's internal vendor master identifier

required_fields.name: invoice_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the invoice document bytes; binds approval to the exact document seen

optional_fields.name: due_date
optional_fields.notes: ISO 8601 date, YYYY-MM-DD

types.digest_notes: invoice_digest defeats invoice-swap fraud between approval and payment

types.summary: Onboarding a vendor into the vendor master, establishing payment rails. A common target of payment-diversion fraud.

required_fields.name: vendor_legal_name
required_fields.notes: registered legal name; organizational names are public record

required_fields.name: jurisdiction
required_fields.notes: country of registration

required_fields.name: tax_id_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized tax identifier (EIN, VAT number); tax IDs are sensitive and never appear raw

required_fields.name: bank_account_digest
required_fields.notes: sha256:<lowercase hex> of the normalized remit-to bank account identifier; the field fraud aims to swap

optional_fields.name: payment_terms
optional_fields.notes: e.g. net-30
